title: ansys-tools-path: A tool to locate Ansys products
section: ansys-tools-path: A tool to locate Ansys products
text:
In order to install ansys-tools-path, make sure you
have the latest version of pip. To do so, run:
Then, you can simply execute:
You can use any of the functions available in the ref_api
to identify the path of the local Ansys installation.
For example you can use find_ansys
to locate the path of the latest Ansys installation available:
python -m pip install -U pip
python -m pip install ansys-tools-path
>>> from ansys.tools.path import find_ansys
>>> find_ansys()
'C:/Program Files/ANSYS Inc/v211/ANSYS/bin/winx64/ansys211.exe', 21.1

title: Contributing
text:
At least two installation modes are provided: user and developer.
In order to install ansys-tools-path, make sure you
have the latest version of pip. To do so, run:
Then, you can simply execute:
Installing ansys-tools-path in developer mode allows
you to modify the source and enhance it.
Before contributing to the project, please refer to the PyAnsys Developer’s guide.
You need to follow these steps:
Start by cloning this repository:
Create a fresh-clean Python environment and activate it:
Make sure you have the latest required build system and doc, testing, and CI tools:
Install the project in editable mode:
If required, you can always call the style commands (black, isort,
flake8…) or unit testing ones (pytest) from the command line. However,
this does not guarantee that your project is being tested in an isolated
environment, which is the reason why tools like tox exist.
The style checks take advantage of pre-commit. Developers are not forced but
encouraged to install this tool via:
For building documentation, you can either run the usual rules provided in the
Sphinx Makefile, such us:
If you would like to create either source or wheel files, start by installing
the building requirements and then executing the build module:
python -m pip install -U pip
python -m pip install ansys-tools-path
git clone https://github.com/ansys/ansys-tools-path
# Create a virtual environment
python -m venv .venv

# Activate it in a POSIX system
source .venv/bin/activate

# Activate it in Windows CMD environment
.venv\Scripts\activate.bat

# Activate it in Windows Powershell
.venv\Scripts\Activate.ps1
python -m pip install .[tests]
python -m pip install .[doc]
python -m pip install .[build]
python -m pip install --editable ansys-tools-path
python -m pip install pre-commit && pre-commit install
make -C doc/ html && your_browser_name doc/html/index.html
python -m pip install .[build]
python -m build
python -m twine check dist/*

title: Contributing > For developers
text:
Installing ansys-tools-path in developer mode allows
you to modify the source and enhance it.
Before contributing to the project, please refer to the PyAnsys Developer’s guide.
You need to follow these steps:
Start by cloning this repository:
Create a fresh-clean Python environment and activate it:
Make sure you have the latest required build system and doc, testing, and CI tools:
Install the project in editable mode:
git clone https://github.com/ansys/ansys-tools-path
# Create a virtual environment
python -m venv .venv

# Activate it in a POSIX system
source .venv/bin/activate

# Activate it in Windows CMD environment
.venv\Scripts\activate.bat

# Activate it in Windows Powershell
.venv\Scripts\Activate.ps1
python -m pip install .[tests]
python -m pip install .[doc]
python -m pip install .[build]
python -m pip install --editable ansys-tools-path

title: Contributing > How to testing
text:
If required, you can always call the style commands (black, isort,
flake8…) or unit testing ones (pytest) from the command line. However,
this does not guarantee that your project is being tested in an isolated
environment, which is the reason why tools like tox exist.
The style checks take advantage of pre-commit. Developers are not forced but
encouraged to install this tool via:
python -m pip install pre-commit && pre-commit install

title: Contributing > A note on pre-commit
text:
The style checks take advantage of pre-commit. Developers are not forced but
encouraged to install this tool via:
python -m pip install pre-commit && pre-commit install

title: Contributing > Documentation
text:
For building documentation, you can either run the usual rules provided in the
Sphinx Makefile, such us:
make -C doc/ html && your_browser_name doc/html/index.html

title: Contributing > Distributing
text:
If you would like to create either source or wheel files, start by installing
the building requirements and then executing the build module:
python -m pip install .[build]
python -m build
python -m twine check dist/*

title: get_available_ansys_installations
text:
Return a dictionary of available Ansys unified installation versions with their base paths.
Return all Ansys unified installations paths in Windows.
On Windows, It uses the environment variable AWP_ROOTXXX.
The student versions are returned at the end of the dict and
with negative value for the version.
Return all installed Ansys paths in Linux.

title: API
text:
find_ansys([version, supported_versions])
Obsolete method, use find_mapdl.
get_available_ansys_installations([...])
Return a dictionary of available Ansys unified installation versions with their base paths.
change_default_ansys_path(exe_loc)
Deprecated, use change_default_mapdl_path instead
save_ansys_path([exe_loc, allow_prompt])
Deprecated, use save_mapdl_path instead
